sections.paragraph:
& לְבֹונָה (Gr. λίβανος, λιβανωτός, [Arab. لُباَنُ Syr. ܠܒܘܽܢܬܳܐ], f.

(1) frankincense, Lev 2:1, 15 5:11 24:7 Num 5:15 Isa 60:6, etc., so called from the white colour of the purest frankincense (Plin. H.N. xii. 14). It is spoken of as growing, not only in Arabia ( Isa 60:6 Jer 6:20), but also in Palestine ( Son 4:6, 14 ), unless in the Canticles some other odoriferous herb is intended.

(2) [Lebonah], pr.n. of a town near Shiloh, only mentioned Jud. 21:19 [now prob. El Lubban اللبّن, Rob. iii. 90].
